extractions.problem: Understanding phenotypic variability in LQT3 hiPSC-CMs and evaluating antiarrhythmic drug responses (mexiletine, ranolazine) using in silico population models.
extractions.method: Populations of in silico hiPSC-CM models (modified Paci2015) calibrated with experimental data; simulated mexiletine and ranolazine multichannel effects at 5/10/20 μM using single pore block model.
extractions.finding: LQT3 models reproduce APD prolongation (+34.3%) matching experiments; symptomatic phenotypes show overexpression of ICaL, IK1, INaL and underexpression of IKr; mexiletine terminates spontaneous APs in far more models than ranolazine (784 vs 53 out of 1,401).
extractions.dataset: LQT3 V1763M mutation hiPSC-CMs, control n=1,463 models, mutant n=1,401 models
extractions.metric: APD90, APD prolongation (%), AP termination count
extractions.baseline: Control hiPSC-CM population, experimental data from Ma et al.
extractions.result: ΔAPD90=+34.3% (sim) vs +48% (exp); mexiletine 784/1401 vs ranolazine 53/1401 AP termination

extractions.problem: Determining the tissue origins of circulating cell-free DNA (cfDNA) in health and disease using an unbiased methylation deconvolution approach.
extractions.method: Reference methylation atlas of 25 human tissues/cell types from Illumina 450K/EPIC arrays; quadratic programming deconvolution of genome-wide cfDNA methylomes; validated with in silico simulations and in vitro DNA mixes at known proportions.
extractions.finding: Healthy plasma cfDNA originates from WBCs (55%), erythrocyte progenitors (30%), vascular endothelial cells (10%), hepatocytes (1%); disease-specific tissue contributions detected in sepsis, islet transplantation, and multiple cancer types agreeing with clinical findings.
extractions.dataset: 25 human tissues/cell types methylation atlas, plasma cfDNA from healthy donors and patients (sepsis, islet transplant, colon/lung/breast/prostate cancer)
extractions.baseline: In vitro DNA mixes at known proportions, in silico simulations
extractions.result: WBCs 55%, erythrocyte progenitors 30%, endothelial 10%, hepatocytes 1% in healthy plasma

extractions.problem: Identifying effective anti-cancer stem cell (CSC) agents to overcome treatment resistance and disease progression in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).
extractions.method: Connectivity Map (CMap) analysis with CSC-associated gene signatures querying 11,641 L1000 profiles and LINCS database (1.3M profiles); validated with in vitro CSC growth/EMT assays and in vivo xenograft models.
extractions.finding: Thiostrepton identified as top anti-CSC candidate; inhibits CSC growth, reduces CD133/Nanog/Oct4A, suppresses EMT and Src signaling, elevates tumor suppressor miR-98; synergistic with gemcitabine in suppressing NSCLC growth.
extractions.dataset: NSCLC cell lines (A549, H460, H1299, CL141, CL152), 11,641 L1000 profiles, CMap 2.0 (6,100 profiles), LINCS (1.3M profiles)
extractions.metric: Normalized enrichment score (NES), connectivity score, P-value
extractions.baseline: CMap database ranking, LINCS database ranking, anti-CSC gene signature (GSE18150)

extractions.problem: Bridging the gap between in vitro cell culture data and clinical outcomes in drug development by integrating novel cell culture technologies with computational in silico models.
extractions.method: Review of hiPSCs, 3D cells, organoids, and microphysiological systems combined with PBPK, PK/PD, QSP models and virtual clinical trials for in vitro to in vivo translation.
extractions.finding: Synergizing high-quality in vitro data from novel cell culture platforms with mathematical models (PBPK, QSP) can better guide drug development, predict clinical outcomes, and optimize dose regimens.

extractions.problem: Automating detection of leukemic cell populations in multi-parameter flow cytometry readouts, replacing labor-intensive and subjective manual gating of low-dimensional projections.
extractions.method: Support Vector Machines (SVM) with RBF kernel for multivariate classification of 4-color flow cytometry data (CD10, CD20, CD34, CD19); trained on manually gated data with sample-wise normalization.
extractions.finding: SVM classification recovers manually gated leukemic cells with high accuracy; multivariate classification captures higher-order marker dependencies missed by 1D/2D gating and enables reliable detection of small cell populations.
extractions.dataset: 37 leukemic samples from childhood precursor B-cell ALL (d0/d8/d15) + 6 artificial dilution mixes, 4-color flow cytometry

extractions.problem: Elucidating the molecular bases of how V1/V2 and V3 loops on HIV-1 gp120 modulate viral receptor recognition (CD4, CCR5) and contribute to viral adaptation in growth-restrictive cells.
extractions.method: Homology modeling of full-length glycosylated gp120 in CD4-free and -bound states; 50 ns molecular dynamics simulation (AMBER 10, ff99SB-ILDN + GLYCAM06); cell-based adaptation experiments identifying enhancing substitutions.
extractions.finding: V1/V2 loop settles near V3 for conformational masking of neutralization epitope in CD4-free state and rearranges near CD4 upon binding; adaptation substitutions on receptor binding surfaces in V1/V2 and V3 enhance viral entry with reduced sensitivity to CCR5 antagonist TAK-779.
extractions.dataset: HIV-1 JRFL strain gp120 (GenBank U63632), PDB structures (3JWD, 3IDX, 3U4E, 2QAD, 2B4C, 1MEQ)
extractions.metric: RMSD, inter-residue distances, viral entry/growth efficiency, TAK-779 sensitivity
extractions.result: RMSD plateau after 20 ns; V1/V2-V3 thermodynamically stable arrangement confirmed

extractions.problem: Predicting and optimizing post-printing cellular behavior in 3D bioprinted cancer cell constructs without extensive trial-and-error experimentation.
extractions.method: Cellular automata (CA) model simulating post-printing cell behavior; validated with 3D bioprinted MDA-MB-231 cell-laden gelatin/alginate hydrogel constructs monitored over 11 days (MTT, live/dead, Ki-67).
extractions.finding: CA model successfully simulated 3D bioprinted structure and captured in vitro observations including viability and proliferation dynamics; can predict behavior for different initial cell numbers and bioink formulations without replicating experiments.
extractions.dataset: MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells in 4% gelatin/4% alginate hydrogel, 2-2.5×10^6 cells
extractions.metric: Cell viability (%), proliferation fold-change, Ki-67 index, doubling time
extractions.baseline: 2D cell culture (3× faster doubling time), in vitro measurements

extractions.problem: Evaluating the potential of platinum nanoparticles (PtNPs) as radiation dose enhancers in breast cancer cell lines and understanding cell-type-dependent efficacy.
extractions.method: In vitro experiments (clonogenicity, cell-cycle, oxidative stress, DNA DSBs) and Monte Carlo in silico simulation of ionization processes with 3.2 nm PEGylated PtNPs in T47D and MDA-MB-231 cells irradiated with 137Cs gamma rays.
extractions.finding: No significant radio-enhancement effect of PtNPs in breast cancer cells, unlike previously observed in HeLa cells; simulation predicted only ~3% dose increase near NPs; PtNPs exhibited antioxidant properties that may counteract the radio-enhancement effect.
extractions.dataset: T47D and MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cell lines, 0.5 mM PtNPs, 0-6 Gy gamma irradiation
extractions.metric: Clonogenic survival fraction, dose enhancement ratio, intracellular Pt mass (pg/cell)
extractions.baseline: Radiation alone (without PtNPs), previous HeLa cell study under same conditions
extractions.result: ~3% simulated dose increase near NPs; no significant biological enhancement; Pt uptake 0.5 pg/cell (MDA-MB-231), 0.2 pg/cell (T47D)

extractions.problem: Developing non-invasive DNA methylation biomarkers for detecting NSCLC and other common carcinomas from circulating cell-free DNA in liquid biopsies.
extractions.method: In silico identification of cancer-specific hypermethylated CpG loci from TCGA data; validated with methylation-specific qPCR on plasma cfDNA from NSCLC patients and healthy controls; cross-validated on 8 independent GEO cancer cohorts.
extractions.finding: 10 DNA methylation biomarkers distinguish NSCLC from controls with high accuracy; signal correlates with tumor size and decreases after surgical resection; validated across 8 additional carcinoma types from independent GEO data.
extractions.dataset: NSCLC patient plasma cfDNA, healthy controls, 8 GEO cancer cohorts (n=1,471 tumors), normal blood (n=310, GSE72773), normal tissues (n=571)
extractions.baseline: Normal blood cohort (n=310), respective normal tissue cohorts
extractions.result: AUC=0.956 (clinical cfDNA), AUC=0.987-1.0 (GEO blood ref), AUC=0.972-1.0 (GEO tissue ref), 5-marker subset AUC=0.97

extractions.problem: Reconstructing the core gene regulatory network of OCT4 in human embryonic stem cells by integrating heterogeneous high-throughput functional data.
extractions.method: Integrated analysis of ChIP-on-chip (OCT4/SOX2/NANOG binding), RNAi-mediated OCT4 silencing, and promoter sequence analysis (octamer/SOX-OCT motif scanning) with de novo motif discovery in hESCs.
extractions.finding: Identified a core OCT4 network of 33 target genes important for pluripotency and differentiation; integration increased functional information 1.3-4.7× over individual methods; de novo motif analysis revealed known regulators and novel candidates including C-MYC/MAX heterodimer.
extractions.dataset: Human ESCs (WA09, H1), ChIP-on-chip (308 targets), RNAi expression (1,104 targets)
extractions.metric: Functional enrichment fold (vs individual methods), overlap percentage between methods
extractions.baseline: Individual experimental methods alone (ChIP-on-chip, RNAi, motif analysis separately)
extractions.result: 33 core target genes identified; functional enrichment 1.3-4.7× vs individual methods; <4% overlap between ChIP-on-chip and RNAi
